spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the headline capability the spec promises: coarse tier 0–4 readout. Aligned with the chromestatus motivation.

interactive.rationale: Real controls drive the demo (buttons, selects/inputs, live script) — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is well covered; edge cases / failure modes from the spec (see open questions) are not exercised on this concept page.

references.rationale: No see-also section and no chromestatus.com/feature link on this concept page; relies on the parent feature index for the canonical link. The self-heal script needs it embedded on every page.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

openQuestions.title: Surface the actual Critical-CH negotiation in the response
openQuestions.detail: A real server-adaptive setup needs Critical-CH so the first request also adapts. Annotate the echo response with the Sec-CH-* request headers it received and whether the route is sending Critical-CH back. Otherwise the demo only models the second hit.

openQuestions.title: Embed the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link in this concept page
openQuestions.detail: The self-heal recovery script relies on every concept page carrying the canonical chromestatus link in a see-also or references block. Add it: https://chromestatus.com/feature/5189864286978048

summary: Server-side adaptation via Client-Hints round-trip plus Server-Timing readout. Main gaps: Surface the actual Critical-CH negotiation in the response; Embed the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link in this concept page.
